the training function calculates the prior probabilities of each class and the likelihood of each word given a class. the prediction function uses the trained model to classify new texts by selecting the class with the highest probability. the evaluation function computes the f1 score based on the predicted and true labels, providing a measure of model performance.  

the model's accuracy is determined by comparing the predicted labels with the true labels. the f1 score reflects the model's ability to correctly classify texts while balancing precision and recall. the evaluation process ensures that the model performs well on both classes, even in the presence of class imbalance.
